title: Distinguish non-qualifying third-place teams in group standings
description:
## What to build

In the Groups view, make each group table communicate when its third-place team does not qualify for the Round of 32. Today the third-place row can look like a special/advancing row because it shares the same rank styling as other group positions. The view should instead make non-qualifying third-place teams clearly different, with a muted/gray treatment or similarly restrained out-of-contention state.

This should be driven by the computed tournament model: the top eight teams in the third-place ranking qualify; the remaining four third-place teams do not. The styling should update when predictions change and the third-place ranking changes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] In the Groups view, a third-place row whose group is outside the top eight third-place qualifiers is visibly distinct from qualifying rows, using a gray/muted treatment or equivalent.
- [ ] Third-place rows that do qualify are not styled as out.
- [ ] The distinction is based on the live computed model, so changing predictions can move the out styling to different groups.
- [ ] The out treatment is understandable in the rendered UI and is not only a fragile CSS color change; use an accessible cue such as text, title/aria text, or an existing status pattern if needed.
- [ ] The change does not clutter every group row: first place, second place, and ordinary fourth-place rows should not gain unnecessary status UI.
- [ ] Verification includes Playwright-driven browser checks in the Groups tab with all groups visible, confirming that exactly the non-qualifying third-place rows receive the out treatment for the current projection.
- [ ] Verification includes a Playwright screenshot or DOM/style assertion at desktop width and at a narrow/mobile width.
- [ ] Run the existing project checks/tests after the change.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately


## Required Skills
playwright
close_reason: Done: Groups view now marks only live-model non-qualifying third-place rows with an accessible Out cue. Validation: npm test; static mirror check; Playwright desktop/mobile DOM and screenshot checks.

title: Neutralize third-place ranking badges
description:
## What to build

The third-place ranking table should not inherit the group-standings rank color semantics where ranks 1-2 are green and rank 3 is yellow. In the third-place table, the numbered rank badge is just an ordinal position among third-place teams, so all rank numbers should use the same neutral badge treatment unless another ticket deliberately adds qualification/out styling around the whole row.

Keep this scoped to the third-place ranking table. The main group standings may continue to use their existing rank styling for group positions unless a separate decision changes that.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] In the third-place ranking table, rank badges for positions 1, 2, 3, and the remaining positions render with the same neutral visual treatment.
- [ ] The main group standings do not regress: group rows can still use the current rank styling for first, second, and third place.
- [ ] The implementation prevents generic `.rank-1`, `.rank-2`, and `.rank-3` styling from leaking into the third-place table.
- [ ] Verification includes Playwright-driven browser inspection of the Third-place tab at desktop width and at a narrow/mobile width.
- [ ] Playwright verification checks the rendered page, not only static CSS: confirm the first three third-place rank badges no longer show green/yellow special colors.
- [ ] Run the existing project checks/tests after the styling change.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately


## Required Skills
playwright

title: Remove fixed team-count label from group cards
description:
## What to build

Remove the "4 teams" label from each group card header in the Groups view. Every World Cup 2026 group card has four teams, so the label adds noise without helping the user scan the standings.

Keep the group name/title visible and preserve the card/table layout after the secondary label is removed.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Group card headers no longer show "4 teams" or an equivalent fixed team-count label.
- [ ] The group title remains visible and aligned cleanly after the label is removed.
- [ ] Filtering to a single group and showing all groups both render without header layout gaps.
- [ ] No other meaningful group metadata is removed.
- [ ] Verification includes Playwright checking the Groups tab for absence of the team-count label.
- [ ] Verification includes a Playwright screenshot or layout check at desktop and narrow/mobile width.
- [ ] Run the existing project checks/tests after the change.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately


## Required Skills
playwright

title: Remove prediction source chips from fixture cards
description:
## What to build

Remove the small source chip from prediction fixture cards, such as "Odds" and "Manual". In the predictions panel these chips are unnecessary visual clutter; the user primarily needs the teams, editable score, fixture timing/group, and concise prediction summary.

Do not remove the underlying prediction source data, because it is still needed for behavior such as preserving manual overrides and reseeding odds predictions. This ticket is display-only unless a small rendering refactor is needed.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Prediction fixture cards no longer display visible "Odds", "Manual", or equivalent source chips.
- [ ] Underlying prediction source behavior remains intact: manual edits still persist as manual overrides and odds reseeding/sync logic still distinguishes odds-backed predictions.
- [ ] Fixture timing/group metadata remains visible.
- [ ] Removing the chip does not leave awkward gaps in the fixture card metadata row.
- [ ] Verification includes Playwright checking the predictions panel for absence of source chips on odds-backed and manually edited fixtures where feasible.
- [ ] Verification includes a Playwright screenshot or layout check at desktop and narrow/mobile width.
- [ ] Run the existing project checks/tests after the change.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately


## Required Skills
playwright

title: Simplify prediction summary copy
description:
## What to build

Simplify fixture prediction summary text in the predictions panel. The current copy repeats information, for example "Favored: Portugal | Portugal 3-0 Uzbekistan" says Portugal twice even though the scoreline already identifies the predicted winner.

Use a concise summary that preserves the predicted scoreline and avoids redundant favored-team text. Draws should remain understandable without adding clutter.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Winning predictions no longer render redundant text like "Favored: Portugal | Portugal 3-0 Uzbekistan".
- [ ] The predicted scoreline remains visible for each predicted fixture.
- [ ] Draw predictions remain clear and concise.
- [ ] Pending predictions still show an appropriate pending state.
- [ ] Manual and odds-backed predictions use the same simplified summary format.
- [ ] Verification includes Playwright checking at least one predicted fixture in the predictions panel for the new concise text.
- [ ] Verification includes a Playwright screenshot or layout check at desktop and narrow/mobile width.
- [ ] Run the existing project checks/tests after the change.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately


## Required Skills
playwright

title: Remove redundant tournament data loaded status
description:
## What to build

Remove the redundant "Tournament data loaded." status message from the prediction/data status area. Once the page has successfully loaded the local tournament snapshot, the steady-state UI should not spend visible space restating that data loaded successfully.

Keep meaningful transient and error states: loading, refresh failures, odds refresh status, and actionable warnings should still appear when relevant.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] After a normal successful load from the local/static data path, the UI does not show "Tournament data loaded.".
- [ ] Error messages still appear if tournament data fails to load.
- [ ] GitHub refresh and odds refresh progress/status messages still appear when those workflows run.
- [ ] The removal does not leave awkward empty spacing or an orphaned status container in the prediction panel.
- [ ] Verification includes Playwright checking the initial rendered page for absence of the redundant message.
- [ ] Verification includes a Playwright screenshot or layout check at desktop and narrow/mobile width.
- [ ] Run the existing project checks/tests after the change.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately


## Required Skills
playwright

title: Trial emoji flags in one standings surface
description:
## What to build

Add a restrained first-pass emoji flag treatment in one standings surface only, then verify whether it improves scanability or creates clutter. Do not roll emoji flags out across fixtures, bracket slots, chips, and every repeated country name in the app as part of this ticket.

Vetting note: emoji flags are likely disruptive if used everywhere because the app already shows team logos/crests in several places, flags render differently by platform, and some labels may represent placeholders or non-standard country names. A good first slice is a controlled standings surface where the flag sits next to the country name and can be judged in context.

Suggested initial surface: third-place ranking team cells, because it is a focused 12-row table and the user is already reviewing that area. If the implementation finds a better single surface while preserving the same restraint, document that choice in the issue notes or PR description.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Emoji flags appear in exactly one initial app surface, not everywhere a country name appears.
- [ ] The implementation uses a maintainable country-to-flag source or helper rather than scattering literal emoji across rendering code.
- [ ] Unknown, placeholder, or ambiguous teams do not show an incorrect flag; omitting the emoji is preferred over guessing wrong.
- [ ] The flag treatment does not duplicate or crowd existing team logos so badly that names become harder to scan.
- [ ] Playwright verification captures desktop and narrow/mobile screenshots of the chosen surface with flags rendered.
- [ ] Playwright verification includes a quick clutter check: team names remain readable, columns do not overflow incoherently, and no controls/text overlap after flags are added.
- [ ] The PR or issue notes explicitly state whether the result should stay limited to that one surface or is safe to consider for a later wider rollout.
- [ ] Run the existing project checks/tests after the change.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately


## Required Skills
playwright
